label: Auto case 1

question: Walk me through your resume and the main roles that are relevant for this position.
response: Sure. I had one job where I did coding, then another job where I did more coding. There were APIs and databases. I also helped with deployments sometimes. I do not really think job titles matter that much, but I have experience.

label: Auto case 3

response: I have looked at Go code. I would say I understand it generally. I have not written a lot of it from scratch, but code is code. If I needed to use it, I would pick it up.

label: Auto case 4
classification_reasoning: Requires a past example of project leadership under pressure
question: Tell me about a time you led a project under a tight deadline.
response: There was a project that had to be done fast. I helped the team get it done. We all worked hard and delivered it. I do not remember the exact timeline, but it was stressful.

follow_ups.question: What was the project and what did you personally own?
follow_ups.response: It was something with an internal tool. I was involved in meetings and helped with some implementation. The team owned most of it together, so I would not say there was one specific thing that was only mine.

label: Auto case 5

response: I usually do not have trouble persuading people. I just explain my view and people understand. I cannot think of a specific example right now, but I am good at communication.

follow_ups.question: Can you give me one actual example?
follow_ups.response: Maybe there was a time a manager did not agree with an estimate. I said it would take longer. I think they accepted it eventually. I do not remember what project it was.

label: Auto case 6
classification_reasoning: Directly prompts for a past example of conflict resolution
question: Tell me about a time you had a conflict with a teammate.
response: I try to avoid conflict. If someone disagrees with me, I usually just let it go unless it is really important. There was a teammate who wrote bad code once, and I told them it was not good. They did not like that, but I was right.

follow_ups.question: How did you handle that conversation?
follow_ups.response: I put comments on the pull request. I probably said it directly. I do not like wasting time sugarcoating things. Eventually someone else fixed the code.
